24	class TestJsonSchemaConstraint(unittest.TestCase):
25	    """Test JSON schema constraint generation for tool choices"""
26	
27	    def setUp(self):
28	        """Set up test tools"""
29	        self.tools = [
30	            Tool(
31	                type="function",
32	                function=Function(
33	                    name="get_weather",
34	                    description="Get weather information",
35	                    parameters={
36	                        "type": "object",
37	                        "properties": {
38	                            "location": {
39	                                "type": "string",
40	                                "description": "Location to get weather for",
41	                            },
42	                            "unit": {
43	                                "type": "string",
44	                                "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
45	                                "description": "Temperature unit",
46	                            },
47	                        },
48	                        "required": ["location"],
49	                    },
50	                ),
51	            ),
52	            Tool(
53	                type="function",
54	                function=Function(
55	                    name="search",
56	                    description="Search for information",
57	                    parameters={
58	                        "type": "object",
59	                        "properties": {
60	                            "query": {
61	                                "type": "string",
62	                                "description": "Search query",
63	                            },
64	                        },
65	                        "required": ["query"],
66	                    },
67	                ),
68	            ),
69	        ]
70	
71	    def test_required_tool_choice_schema(self):
72	        """Test schema generation for tool_choice='required'"""
73	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, "required")
74	
75	        self.assertIsNotNone(schema)
76	        jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema(schema)
77	
78	        self.assertEqual(schema["type"], "array")
79	        self.assertEqual(schema["minItems"], 1)
80	        self.assertIn("items", schema)
81	        self.assertIn("anyOf", schema["items"])
82	
83	        # Should have schemas for both tools
84	        self.assertEqual(len(schema["items"]["anyOf"]), 2)
85	
86	        # Check that each tool schema is present
87	        tool_names = [
88	            item["properties"]["name"]["enum"][0] for item in schema["items"]["anyOf"]
89	        ]
90	        self.assertIn("get_weather", tool_names)
91	        self.assertIn("search", tool_names)
92	
93	    def test_specific_tool_choice_schema(self):
94	        """Test schema generation for specific tool choice"""
95	        tool_choice = ToolChoice(
96	            type="function", function=ToolChoiceFuncName(name="get_weather")
97	        )
98	        schema = get_json_schema_constraint(self.tools, tool_choice)
99	
100	        self.assertIsNotNone(schema)

288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:

291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)

307	                if isinstance(format_dict, dict):
308	                    self._sanitize_structural_format(format_dict)

311	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
312	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:

324	    from transformers import AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer
325

327	
328	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
329	    hf_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
330	
331	    # Should use vocab size from model config
332	    vocab_size = hf_config.vocab_size
333	    eos_token_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
334	
335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()
353	
354

9	    def _run_decode_ebnf(
10	        self,
11	        ebnf,
12	        expected_patterns,
13	        prompt,
14	        return_logprob=False,
15	        top_logprobs_num=0,
16	        n=1,
17	    ):
18	        response = requests.post(
19	            self.base_url + "/generate",
20	            json={
21	                "text": prompt,
22	                "sampling_params": {
23	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
24	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
25	                    "n": n,
26	                    "ebnf": ebnf,
27	                },
28	                "stream": False,
29	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
30	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
31	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
32	            },
33	        )
34	
35	        ret = response.json()
36	        print(json.dumps(ret, indent=2))
37	        print("=" * 100)
38	
39	        if not isinstance(ret, list):
40	            self.fail(f"Expected response to be a list, but got {type(ret)}")
41	
42	        for item in ret:
43	            text = item.get("text", "").strip()
44	            if not text:
45	                self.fail("Generated text is empty.")
46	
47	            match = False
48	            for pattern in expected_patterns:
49	                if self.regex_match(text, pattern):
50	                    match = True
51	                    break
52	            if not match:
53	                self.fail(f"Text '{text}' does not match any of the allowed patterns.")
54	
55	    def regex_match(self, text, pattern):
56	        import re
57	
58	        return re.match(pattern, text) is not None

53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")
120	
121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
123	            self.ctx,
124	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
126	        )
127	        return XGrammarGrammar(
128	            matcher,
129	            self.vocab_size,
130	            self.ctx,
131	            self.override_stop_tokens,
132	            self.key_string,
133	            dataclasses.replace(
134	                self.grammar_stats, is_cache_hit=True, tree_traversal_time=[]
135	            ),
136	        )

12	def _find_common_prefix(s1: str, s2: str) -> str:
13	    prefix = ""
14	    min_length = min(len(s1), len(s2))
15	    for i in range(0, min_length):
16	        if s1[i] == s2[i]:
17	            prefix += s1[i]
18	        else:
19	            break
20	    return prefix

23	def _partial_json_loads(input_str: str, flags: Allow) -> Tuple[Any, int]:
24	    """
25	    Parse incomplete or partial JSON strings commonly encountered during streaming.
26	
27	    Args:
28	        input_str (str): The potentially incomplete JSON string to parse.
29	        flags (Allow): Bitwise flags controlling what types of partial data are allowed.
30	            Common flags include:
31	            - Allow.STR: Allow partial strings (e.g., '"hello wo' -> 'hello wo')
32	            - Allow.OBJ: Allow partial objects (e.g., '{"key":' -> {'key': None})
33	            - Allow.ARR: Allow partial arrays (e.g., '[1, 2,' -> [1, 2])
34	            - Allow.ALL: Allow all types of partial data
35	
36	    Returns:
37	        Tuple[Any, int]: A tuple containing:
38	            - parsed_object: The Python object parsed from the JSON
39	            - consumed_length: Number of characters consumed from input_str
40	    """
41	    try:
42	        return (partial_json_parser.loads(input_str, flags), len(input_str))
43	    except (JSONDecodeError, IndexError) as e:
44	        msg = getattr(e, "msg", str(e))
45	        if "Extra data" in msg or "pop from empty list" in msg:
46	            start = WHITESPACE.match(input_str, 0).end()
47	            obj, end = JSONDecoder().raw_decode(input_str, start)
48	            return obj, end
49	        raise
50	
51	
52	def _is_complete_json(input_str: str) -> bool:
53	    try:
54	        orjson.loads(input_str)
55	        return True
56	    except JSONDecodeError:
57	        return False

60	def _get_tool_schema_defs(tools: List[Tool]) -> dict:
61	    """
62	    Get consolidated $defs from all tools, validating for conflicts.
63	
64	    Args:
65	        tools: List of tools to process
66	
67	    Returns:
68	        Dictionary of consolidated $defs from all tools
69	
70	    Raises:
71	        ValueError: If conflicting $defs are found
72	    """
73	    all_defs = {}
74	    for tool in tools:
75	        if tool.function.parameters is None:
76	            continue
77	        defs = tool.function.parameters.get("$defs", {})
78	        for def_name, def_schema in defs.items():
79	            if def_name in all_defs and all_defs[def_name] != def_schema:
80	                raise ValueError(
81	                    f"Tool definition '{def_name}' has "
82	                    "multiple schemas, which is not "
83	                    "supported."
84	                )
85	            else:
86	                all_defs[def_name] = def_schema
87	    return all_defs

25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total
244

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):

131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(

134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)

Now I have comprehensive information about the SGLang constraint system. Let me compile my findings:

## Summary: JSON Schema and Regex Constraints in SGLang Codebase

I've thoroughly analyzed the SGLang codebase to understand the constraint implementation architecture. Here are my findings:

### 1. **Constraint Files and Architecture**

**Core Implementation Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775323786369-vb8dsh/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` - Orchestrates grammar processing and caching
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775323786369-vb8dsh/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` - Abstract base class defining the constraint plugin architecture
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775323786369-vb8dsh/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` - Outlines library integration
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775323786369-vb8dsh/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` - XGrammar library integration (primary backend)

**Sampling/IR Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775323786369-vb8dsh/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` - Defines constraint parameter types
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775323786369-vb8dsh/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` - IR representation with constraint support

---

### 2. **Constraint Type System**

**Defined in `SamplingParams` class:**
```python
# From sampling_params.py (lines 55-58)
json_schema: Optional[str] = None
regex: Optional[str] = None
ebnf: Optional[str] = None
structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
```

**Currently Supported Constraint Types (in grammar_manager.py lines 70-87):**
1. `json_schema` - JSON schema constraint
2. `regex` - Regular expression constraint
3. `ebnf` - Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar
4. `structural_tag` - Structured output with tags

**Mutual Exclusivity (sampling_params.py lines 155-161):**
Only one constraint can be set at a time. The validation checks this.

---

### 3. **Constraint Processing Pipeline**

**Three-Tier Architecture:**

1. **Language API Layer** (`sglang/lang/api.py` and `ir.py`):
   - User-facing `gen()` function accepts `regex` and `json_schema` parameters
   - Creates `SglGen` object with `SglSamplingParams`
   - Example: `sgl.gen("output", max_tokens=128, regex=pattern)`

2. **Sampling Parameters Layer** (`sampling_params.py`):
   - `SamplingParams` class holds constraint as string
   - `verify()` method checks mutual exclusivity
   - `normalize()` method preprocesses stop regex patterns

3. **Grammar Manager Layer** (`grammar_manager.py`):
   - `process_req_with_grammar()` creates cache keys as tuples: `("json", schema_string)`, `("regex", regex_string)`, etc.
   - Dispatches to appropriate backend
   - Uses thread pool executor for concurrent grammar compilation
   - Returns `BaseGrammarObject` with vocab masking interface

---

### 4. **Backend Dispatch Architecture** (`base_grammar_backend.py`)

**BaseGrammarBackend Class Structure:**
- Abstract base with dispatcher methods:
  - `dispatch_json(key_string)` → processes JSON schema
  - `dispatch_regex(key_string)` → processes regex pattern
  - `dispatch_ebnf(key_string)` → processes EBNF grammar
  - `dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)` → processes structural tags
  - `dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)` → *(stub for future expansion)*
  - `dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)` → *(stub for future expansion)*
  - `dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)` → error handling

**Key Pattern - Router in `_init_value_dispatch()` (lines 157-179):**
```python
if key_type == "json":
    grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
elif key_type == "regex":
    grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
# ... more types ...
elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
    grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
    grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
else:
    grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
```

---

### 5. **XGrammar Backend Implementation** (Primary Backend)

**Key Classes:**
- `XGrammarGrammar` - Wraps XGrammar `GrammarMatcher` for token acceptance
- `XGrammarGrammarBackend` - Implements all dispatch methods

**JSON Processing (lines 257-270):**
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
    else:
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
            schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
        )
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
```

**Regex Processing (lines 280-286):**
```python
def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
    ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
    return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
```

**Token-to-Vocab Masking Interface:**
- `allocate_vocab_mask()` - Creates bitmask tensor
- `fill_vocab_mask()` - Updates allowed tokens at current state
- `apply_vocab_mask()` - Applies mask to logits (CPU/CUDA/HIP)

---

### 6. **Outlines Backend Integration**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775323786369-vb8dsh/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`

**Key Difference from XGrammar:**
- Uses `RegexGuide` from outlines library
- Converts JSON schema to regex via `build_regex_from_schema()` (imported from outlines or outlines_core)
- Then applies regex constraint

**JSON-to-Regex Pattern (lines 166-175):**
```python
def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
    regex = build_regex_from_object(
        key_string,
        whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
    )
    return self._compile_regex(regex)
```

---

### 7. **Constraint Validation Logic**

**In `sampling_params.py`:**
1. Mutual exclusivity check (lines 155-161)
2. Regex validation in `gen()` via `re.compile()` (api.py lines 111-115)
3. Max sequence length calculation from regex patterns

---

### 8. **Grammar Caching & Threading**

**Cache Entry Structure (base_grammar_backend.py lines 123-126):**
```python
@dataclass
class CacheEntry:
    value: BaseGrammarObject
    event: Event
```

**Caching Strategy (grammar_manager.py lines 89-96):**
- Key: tuple of `(constraint_type, constraint_string)`
- Async compilation via `ThreadPoolExecutor`
- Future-based waiting with configurable timeout
- Hit detection before compilation

---

### 9. **Constraint Flow to Tokenizer/Decoding**

**State Management in BaseGrammarObject (base_grammar_backend.py):**
```python
def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
    """Accept a token in the grammar."""
    raise NotImplementedError()

def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
    """Update which tokens are allowed at current state"""
    raise NotImplementedError()

def is_terminated(self):
    """Check if grammar allows more tokens"""
    return False
```

**Integration Point:**
- Logit processor receives vocab mask after grammar state update
- Applied before sampling: `logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))`

---

### 10. **No Prior JSONPath Work Found**

Search results for `jsonpath` and `json_path` only returned matches in:
- `/sgl-model-gateway/e2e_test/benchmarks/summarize.py` (unrelated to constraints)
- `/python/sglang/srt/model_loader/ci_weight_validation.py` (unrelated to constraints)
- `/python/sglang/multimodal_gen/benchmarks/bench_serving.py` (unrelated to constraints)

**Conclusion:** No existing JSONPath constraint implementation in the codebase.

---

### 11. **Integration Points for New "json_path" Constraint**

**Required Modifications:**

1. **Add to `SamplingParams`** (sampling_params.py):
   - Add `json_path: Optional[str] = None` parameter
   - Update mutual exclusivity check to include new type

2. **Add to `SglSamplingParams`** (ir.py):
   - Add `json_path` field
   - Update `to_srt_kwargs()` method

3. **Add to `SglGen`** (ir.py):
   - Add `json_path` parameter to constructor

4. **Add to user API** (api.py):
   - Add `json_path` parameter to `gen()` function

5. **Add to `GrammarManager`** (grammar_manager.py):
   - Update `process_req_with_grammar()` to handle `json_path` constraint type
   - Add line: `elif req.sampling_params.json_path is not None: key = ("json_path", req.sampling_params.json_path)`

6. **Add to Backend Base Class** (base_grammar_backend.py):
   - Add dispatch method: `def dispatch_json_path(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]`
   - Update `_init_value_dispatch()` to route `"json_path"` type

7. **Implement in XGrammar Backend** (xgrammar_backend.py):
   - Add `dispatch_json_path()` implementation using XGrammar's JSON path support

8. **Implement in Outlines Backend** (outlines_backend.py):
   - Add stub or alternative implementation for `dispatch_json_path()`

---

### 12. **Example Usage Patterns**

**Regex Constraint Example** (`examples/frontend_language/usage/chinese_regex.py`):
```python
@sgl.function
def character_gen(s, name):
    s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)
```

**JSON Schema Example** (`examples/frontend_language/usage/json_decode.py`):
```python
s += sgl.gen(
    "character",
    max_tokens=128,
    regex=build_regex_from_object(Wizard),  # Pydantic model
)
```

**Test Examples** (`python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py`, `json_constrained_kit.py`):
- Uses HTTP `/generate` endpoint with `sampling_params` dict
- Validates output matches constraint pattern

---

### Key Architectural Insights:

1. **Plugin Architecture**: Backend system uses registry pattern (`GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY`) allowing custom backends
2. **Lazy Compilation**: Grammars compiled in background threads, cached by key
3. **Tokenizer Integration**: Constraints work at vocab mask level, not token-by-token validation
4. **State Machine**: Each grammar object maintains acceptance state, allows rollback
5. **Type Safety**: Mutual exclusivity enforced at multiple levels
6. **Two-Backend Strategy**: XGrammar (primary) + Outlines (fallback) for compatibility

This architecture is well-designed for adding new constraint types like "json_path" with minimal modifications.

4. **State Machine**: Each grammar object maintains acceptance state, allows rollback
5. **Type Safety**: Mutual exclusivity enforced at multiple levels
6. **Two-Backend Strategy**: XGrammar (primary) + Outlines (fallback) for compatibility

This architecture is well-designed for adding new constraint types like "json_path" with minimal modifications.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report:

## SGLang Output Constraints - Comprehensive Implementation Overview

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a complete analysis of how output constraints are implemented:

### 1. OVERALL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Key constraint-related directories:**
- `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/` - Core constraint backend implementations
- `/python/sglang/lang/` - User-facing API and IR definitions
- `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/` - Sampling parameters and batch info
- `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/` - OpenAI API integration

### 2. ALL EXISTING CONSTRAINT IMPLEMENTATIONS

The framework supports **4 main constraint types**:

1. **JSON Schema Constraints** - Ensures output matches a JSON schema
2. **Regex Constraints** - Ensures output matches a regular expression pattern
3. **EBNF Constraints** - Extended Backus-Naur Form grammar
4. **Structural Tags** - For specialized structured output (e.g., reasoning patterns)

**Available Backend Implementations:**

| Backend | Location | Supported Constraints |
|---------|----------|----------------------|
| **XGrammar** | `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | JSON, Regex, EBNF, Structural Tags |
| **Outlines** | `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | JSON, Regex |
| **LLGuidance** | `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` | JSON, Regex, EBNF |
| **Base/None** | `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | No constraints |

**Special Backends:**
- `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/reasoner_grammar_backend.py` - Wraps other backends for reasoning mode support

### 3. HOW CONSTRAINTS ARE DEFINED

**A. In IR (Intermediate Representation)**
File: `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

```python
class SglSamplingParams:
    def __init__(self, ...):
        self.regex: Optional[str] = None              # Regex pattern string
        self.json_schema: Optional[str] = None        # JSON schema as string
        self.dtype: Optional[str] = None              # Type constraint (e.g., int, str)
```

**B. In Backend Sampling Params**
File: `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

```python
class SamplingParams:
    def __init__(self, ...):
        self.json_schema: Optional[str] = None
        self.regex: Optional[str] = None
        self.ebnf: Optional[str] = None
        self.structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
        # ... other params
    
    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        # Ensures only ONE constraint type is set
        grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

### 4. HOW CONSTRAINTS ARE REGISTERED/EXPORTED

**Registry System:**
File: `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`

```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids):
    # Checks custom registry first
    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](...)
    
    # Then built-in backends
    if name == "outlines":
        return OutlinesGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "xgrammar":
        return XGrammarGrammarBackend(...)
    elif name == "llguidance":
        return GuidanceBackend(...)
```

**Dispatch System** for different constraint types:
```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def _init_value_dispatch(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool):
        key_type, key_string = key
        if key_type == "json":
            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
        elif key_type == "regex":
            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
        elif key_type == "ebnf":
            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
```

### 5. USER-FACING API

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py`

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,           # Add regex constraint
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,     # Add JSON schema constraint
):
    """Call the model to generate"""
    if choices:
        return SglSelect(...)  # For choice constraints
    
    # Validate regex
    if regex is not None:
        try:
            re.compile(regex)
        except re.error as e:
            raise e
    
    return SglGen(
        ...,
        regex=regex,
        json_schema=json_schema,
    )
```

**Also exposed:**
- `select()` - For choice-based constraints (predefined options)
- `gen_int()` - For integer output with type constraint
- `gen_string()` - For string output with type constraint

**File:** `/python/sglang/__init__.py` exports:
```python
from sglang.lang.api import (
    gen,
    gen_int,
    gen_string,
    select,
    ...
)
```

### 6. OPENAI API INTEGRATION

File: `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/serving_completions.py`

```python
# Convert OpenAI response_format to SGLang sampling params
if request.response_format and request.response_format.type == "json_schema":
    sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
        request.response_format.json_schema.schema_
    )

# Also supported:
sampling_params["regex"] = request.regex
sampling_params["structural_tag"] = request.structural_tag
```

### 7. CONSTRAINT FLOW: USER CODE → BACKEND → DECODING

**Flow Diagram:**

```
User Code
    ↓
SglGen (IR with regex/json_schema/ebnf)
    ↓
Interpreter/Runtime
    ↓
SamplingParams (in SRT backend)
    ↓
GrammarManager (process_req_with_grammar)
    ↓
Grammar Backend (XGrammar/Outlines/LLGuidance)
    ↓
BaseGrammarObject (compile to grammar matcher)
    ↓
SamplingBatchInfo.update_regex_vocab_mask()
    ↓
Sampler forward pass
    ↓
apply_vocab_mask() in logits_processor
    ↓
Token sampling (only valid tokens allowed)
```

### 8. CONSTRAINT PROCESSING PIPELINE

**Step 1: Grammar Manager** - `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None):
        
        # Create cache key
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        # ...
        
        # Get or compile grammar (cached)
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(key, ...)
        req.grammar = value
```

**Step 2: Grammar Compilation** (done in thread pool for non-blocking)
Each backend implements:
```python
def dispatch_json(self, json_schema_str: str) -> BaseGrammarObject
def dispatch_regex(self, regex_str: str) -> BaseGrammarObject
def dispatch_ebnf(self, ebnf_str: str) -> BaseGrammarObject
```

**Step 3: Token Masking at Sample Time** - `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`

```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    # Allocate mask for valid tokens
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(
        vocab_size=self.vocab_size,
        batch_size=len(self.temperatures),
        device=self.device,
    )
    
    # Fill mask with allowed tokens per sequence
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished:
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    # Store apply function
    self.apply_mask_func = first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
```

**Step 4: Mask Application** - During sampling

```python
def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

### 9. BACKEND-SPECIFIC IMPLEMENTATIONS

**XGrammar Backend** - `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`

```python
class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
    def __init__(self, matcher: GrammarMatcher, ...):
        self.matcher = matcher  # xgrammar's GrammarMatcher
    
    def accept_token(self, token: int):
        accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int):
        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
        # Uses triton or hip kernel for efficient masking
        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
```

**Outlines Backend** - `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`

```python
class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
    def __init__(self, guide: RegexGuide, ...):
        self.guide = guide      # Outlines FSM guide
        self.state = 0
    
    def accept_token(self, token: int):
        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int):
        tokens = self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens
        vocab_mask[idx].fill_(1)
        vocab_mask[idx].scatter_(0, tokens, False)  # Mark valid tokens
```

### 10. TEST EXAMPLES

**Test Kits** showing user-facing API usage:

**JSON Schema Test** - `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py`
```python
json_schema = json.dumps({
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "name": {"type": "string"},
        "population": {"type": "integer"},
    },
    "required": ["name", "population"],
})

response = requests.post(
    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Introduce the capital of France...",
        "sampling_params": {
            "json_schema": json_schema,  # Constraint passed here
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
        },
    },
)
```

**Regex Test** - `/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py`
```python
response = requests.post(
    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Generate an email address:",
        "sampling_params": {
            "regex": r"^user@example\.com$",  # Constraint passed here
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
        },
    },
)
```

**EBNF Test** - `/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py`
```python
response = requests.post(
    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Generate a phone number:",
        "sampling_params": {
            "ebnf": """
            root ::= "(" area ")" " " prefix "-" line
            area ::= [0-9] [0-9] [0-9]
            ...
            """,  # Constraint passed here
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
        },
    },
)
```

### 11. KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | User-facing API (`gen()`, `select()`, etc.) |
| `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | IR definitions (`SglGen`, `SglSamplingParams`) |
| `python/sglang/__init__.py` | Public exports |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Backend sampling params with constraint validation |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | Abstract base and registry |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Request processing and caching |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | XGrammar backend implementation |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | Outlines backend implementation |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` | LLGuidance backend implementation |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` | Batch-level mask management |
| `test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py` | Integration tests |
| `python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py` | JSON constraint test suite |
| `python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py` | Regex constraint test suite |
| `python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py` | EBNF constraint test suite |

This architecture provides a clean separation: users specify constraints in simple Python code or JSON, which flows through the IR to the backend, where pluggable grammar backends (XGrammar, Outlines, LLGuidance) compile constraints into efficient token masks that guide sampling at decode time.

```python
        self.state = 0
    
    def accept_token(self, token: int):
        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int):
        tokens = self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens
        vocab_mask[idx].fill_(1)
        vocab_mask[idx].scatter_(0, tokens, False)  # Mark valid tokens
```

### 10. TEST EXAMPLES

**Test Kits** showing user-facing API usage:

**JSON Schema Test** - `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py`
```python
json_schema = json.dumps({
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
        "name": {"type": "string"},
        "population": {"type": "integer"},
    },
    "required": ["name", "population"],
})

response = requests.post(
    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Introduce the capital of France...",
        "sampling_params": {
            "json_schema": json_schema,  # Constraint passed here
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
        },
    },
)
```

**Regex Test** - `/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py`
```python
response = requests.post(
    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Generate an email address:",
        "sampling_params": {
            "regex": r"^user@example\.com$",  # Constraint passed here
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
        },
    },
)
```

**EBNF Test** - `/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py`
```python
response = requests.post(
    base_url + "/generate",
    json={
        "text": "Generate a phone number:",
        "sampling_params": {
            "ebnf": """
            root ::= "(" area ")" " " prefix "-" line
            area ::= [0-9] [0-9] [0-9]
            ...
            """,  # Constraint passed here
            "max_new_tokens": 128,
        },
    },
)
```

### 11. KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | User-facing API (`gen()`, `select()`, etc.) |
| `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | IR definitions (`SglGen`, `SglSamplingParams`) |
| `python/sglang/__init__.py` | Public exports |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | Backend sampling params with constraint validation |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` | Abstract base and registry |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` | Request processing and caching |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py` | XGrammar backend implementation |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | Outlines backend implementation |
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/llguidance_backend.py` | LLGuidance backend implementation |
| `python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` | Batch-level mask management |
| `test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py` | Integration tests |
| `python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py` | JSON constraint test suite |
| `python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py` | Regex constraint test suite |
| `python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py` | EBNF constraint test suite |

This architecture provides a clean separation: users specify constraints in simple Python code or JSON, which flows through the IR to the backend, where pluggable grammar backends (XGrammar, Outlines, LLGuidance) compile constraints into efficient token masks that guide sampling at decode time.

203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))

206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

# Copyright 2023-2024 SGLang Team
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================
"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

import logging
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union

# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
try:
    import re._parser as sre_parse
except ImportError:
    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SamplingParams:
    """
    The sampling parameters.

    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
    for the documentation.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
        n: int = 1,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
        self.stop_strs = stop
        if stop_token_ids:
            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
        else:
            self.stop_token_ids = None
        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.top_p = top_p
        self.top_k = top_k
        self.min_p = min_p
        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
        self.regex = regex
        self.n = n
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
        self.custom_params = custom_params
        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed

        # Process some special cases
        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
            self.temperature = 1.0
            self.top_k = 1
        if self.top_k == -1:
            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary

    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
            )
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
            )
        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
                )
            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
                )
        if self.logit_bias is not None:
            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
                        f"{token_id}."
                    )

        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
        # Process stop strings
        if self.stop_strs is None:
            self.stop_strs = []
            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]

            stop_str_max_len = 0
            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
                if tokenizer is not None:
                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
                else:
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len

        # Process stop regex strings
        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
            self.stop_regex_strs = []
            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]

            stop_regex_max_len = 0
            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
                stop_regex_max_len = max(
                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
                )

            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len


# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
# to be buffered to match the input regex string
# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
# token
def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))


MAX_LEN = 2**30


def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
    total = 0
    for token, value in subpattern:
        if token in {
            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
        }:
            total += 1
        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
            # EG: (a\d+) ->
            # [(SUBPATTERN,
            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
            _, branches = value
            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
                total += MAX_LEN
            else:
                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
            # length
            total += 0
        else:
            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")

            total += MAX_LEN

    return total

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from concurrent import futures
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List

import torch

from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
    create_grammar_backend,
)
from sglang.srt.environ import envs

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class GrammarManager:
    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
        self.scheduler = scheduler
        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
                self.server_args,
                scheduler.tokenizer,
                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
            )
        else:
            self.grammar_backend = None

        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank

        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
        )

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.grammar_queue)

    def clear(self):
        if self.grammar_backend:
            self.grammar_backend.reset()

    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0

    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
        for req in self.grammar_queue:
            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
                if req.grammar:
                    req.grammar.cancel()
                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")

    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        # Init grammar cache for this request
        add_to_grammar_queue = False
        if (
            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
        ):
            if self.grammar_backend is None:
                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
            else:
                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)

                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                    key, req.require_reasoning
                )
                req.grammar = value

                if not cache_hit:
                    req.grammar_key = key
                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
                else:
                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        if add_to_grammar_queue:
            self.grammar_queue.append(req)

        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """
        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.

        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)

        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
        """
        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()

        # Poll for ready requests
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
                if i in ready_req_idxs:
                    continue

                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
                    continue

                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
                if req.grammar.done():
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)

            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)

        # Check failed requests
        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
                if (
                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
                ):
                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
        else:
            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
                all_gather_output,
                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
            )
            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])

        # Return ready requests
        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)
            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                continue

            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Return failed requests
        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)

            req.grammar.cancel()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
        self.grammar_queue = [
            req
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
        ]
        return return_reqs

14	"""The baseclass of a backend for grammar-guided constrained decoding."""

16	import logging

32	    compilation_time: Optional[float] = None
33	    schema_count: Optional[int] = None
34	    ebnf_size: Optional[int] = None
35	    is_cache_hit: bool = False
36	    is_grammar_aborted: bool = False

38	    dispatch_type: Optional[str] = None
39	    num_timeout: int = 0

42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51

53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()

59	        raise NotImplementedError()

62	        return False

65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter
88	    def finished(self, finished):
89	        self._finished = finished
90	
91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()
100	
101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:
114	        """
115	        Jump forward occurs, and update the grammar state if needed.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()
118	
119	
120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()
121	
122	
123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197	
198	
199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend
275
